assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Implementation Notes section is missing from the task description and flags this as a gap requiring clarification (constraint 1.6)

assertion_results.text: The response asks at least 3 specific clarifying questions about: which fields to search, what relevance ranking means, and what performance threshold is required
assertion_results.evidence: 1) Which fields to search: Under 'Description' item 3 — 'What fields are searchable (package name, namespace, type, version, license, associated SBOMs)?' and under 'API Changes' — 'What query parameters does it accept? (e.g., q, name, namespace, type, version?)'. 2) What relevance ranking means: Under 'Acceptance Criteria' item 4 — 'What "relevant" means (e.g., exact matches ranked above partial matches)'. 3) What performance threshold is required: Under 'Acceptance Criteria' item 4 — 'Quantitative performance thresholds (e.g., "responds within 200ms for datasets up to 100k packages")'. Additional questions cover search mechanism (SQL LIKE vs full-text), endpoint path/method, response shape, test scenarios, and more — well exceeding the 3-question minimum.
